meaning: n.(说话的,思想的)游移不定,漂泊,流浪,离题
examples: The tramp was arrested for vagrancy.
Two men were arrested and charged with vagrancy.
At one point, he gave a panhandler a chance to make himself scarce for the crime of vagrancy, which was almost un-Dredd-ful.
Under-performance among working-class boys has become a particular concern for the government, because it is associated with high rates of vagrancy and crime.
